page: Amazon Web Services m6in.2xlarge is a general-purpose instance powered by an Intel Xeon 8375C processor with an x86_64 architecture. It features 8 vCPUs mapped to 4 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled, running at a clock speed of 3.5 GHz. The CPU includes 54 MB of L3 cache and supports advanced instruction sets such as AVX-512 and AES cryptography. The instance is equipped with 32.0 GB of DDR4 memory operating at 3 GHz, providing 4.0 GB of RAM per core. It does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware, relying instead on network-attached storage.

Benchmark results place the m6in.2xlarge consistently in the average range for general-purpose workloads. It achieves a Geekbench single-core score of 1625.0 and a multi-core score of 6029.0. Memory bandwidth tests show 414.37 GB/sec for cached small-block reads and 56.93 GB/sec for uncached large-block reads. Database operations, measured via PassMark, score at 3963.55, while Redis SET operations reach 7.93 million operations per second. However, multi-threaded bzip3 decompression and medium-sized LLM inference tasks show weaker performance, with text generation for a 7B model dropping to 7.52 tokens per second.

From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the m6in.2xlarge offers a balanced resource density of 4 GB RAM per vCPU, making it suitable for standard enterprise applications without the premium cost of memory-optimized hardware. The Nitro hypervisor ensures dedicated CPU allocation and a baseline network bandwidth of 12 Gbps. The primary tradeoff is the lack of local NVMe storage, which requires reliance on EBS volumes for disk operations. This instance is best suited for general-purpose workloads, static web serving, software compilation, and lightweight database operations.
description: Amazon Web Services m6in.2xlarge is an x86_64 general-purpose instance powered by an Intel Xeon 8375C processor running at 3.5 GHz. It features 8 vCPUs, 4 physical cores, and 32.0 GB of DDR4 memory, yielding a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per core. The instance lacks local storage and GPUs, relying on the Nitro hypervisor and a baseline network bandwidth of 12 Gbps. Performance benchmarks generally fall in the average tier, with a Geekbench multi-core score of 6029.0 and Redis SET operations reaching 7.93 million operations per second. However, it exhibits weaker performance in multi-threaded decompression and medium-sized LLM inference. This instance provides a balanced resource configuration suitable for general-purpose workloads, static web serving, software compilation, and standard database operations.
